title: nepeta spicata var. elata
intro: Nepeta spicata var. elata, also known as Catmint or Elatum Catmint, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its blue-purple flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Nepeta spicata var. elata include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried flowers and leaves that can be added to smoldering incense or burned as a potpourri.
